center.name: 北開普省
center.summary: 北開普省是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联5种鸟类，包括高山雨燕、Damara tern、Karoo thrush等5种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Cape weaver is endemic to South Africa, Lesotho and Eswatini, occurring across much of the area excluding the Kalahari Desert from the Orange River in the Northern Cape south to the Cape of Good Hope then east to northern KwaZulu Natal and inland almost to Bloemfontein in the Free State. The Cape weaver occurs in open grassland, lowland fynbos, coastal thicket and farmland, so long as there is permanent water and trees. In the more arid, hotter regions it is restricted to upland areas and it never occurs in forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in western coastal Southern Africa from the Eastern Cape through the Western Cape and Northern Cape into Namibia and Angola; 98% of the population of 14,000 individuals nests in Namibia. The majority of non-breeding birds migrate north reaching Benin, Cameroon, Republic of the Congo, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Ivory Coast, Gabon, Ghana, Liberia, Nigeria, and Togo{{cite journal | author = J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It occurs in South Africa, where it is present in Little Namaqualand, the Karoo and Northern Cape, Free State, Gauteng, Limpopo, Mpumalanga and parts of the North West Province

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in South Africa, Namibia, and Botswana, but its range is centered within the Northern Cape Province of South Africa.{{cite book|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20061015081716/http://web.uct.ac.za/depts/stats/adu/pdf/800.pdf|archive-date=2006-10-15|last1= Mendelsohn|first1= J.M.|title= The Atlas of Southern African Birds|year= 1997|publisher= BirdLife South Africa|chapter-url= http://web.uct.ac.za/depts/stats/adu/pdf/800.pdf|last2= Anderson|first2= M.D.|editor= J.A

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Bradfield|R D Bradfield]] (1935) from Quickborn, Damaraland distributed in Namibia and adjacent western South Africa in the northwestern parts of Northern Cape, (7) A
